summary: Moved several build/compiler knobs that were previously hardcoded or marked TODO in `configure.py` into argparse-controlled options, making the Python configuration flow cover Makefile-style controls for Metrowerks diagnostics, matching/nonmatching mode, WIP code, prototype requirements, and warning behavior. Also changed missing config/source warnings to follow `--verbose` rather than `config.debug`.
what_changed: Removed hardcoded/TODO compiler flags from the base flag list, including fixed `-msgstyle gcc`, fixed `-maxerrors 1`, and unconditional `-DMUST_MATCH`.
Reintroduced those flags conditionally from parsed arguments: `-maxerrors`, `-nofail` when max errors is 0, `-msgstyle`, `-warn all`, `-warn iserror`, `-DMUST_MATCH`, `-DWIP`, and `-requireprotos`.
Kept debug behavior for `-sym on`/`-DDEBUG=1` versus `-DNDEBUG=1` unchanged.
Changed `config.warn_missing_config` and `config.warn_missing_source` from being tied to `config.debug` to being tied to `args.verbose`.
smart_moves: Used argparse `choices` for `--msg-style`, limiting compiler message-style values to known supported modes: `mpw`, `std`, `gcc`, `ide`, and `parseable`.

Used negative boolean flags with explicit destinations, e.g. `--no-warn-error` sets `warn_error` false and `--no-require-protos` sets `require_protos` false, preserving strict defaults while giving developers an escape hatch.
Preserved matching builds as the default by appending `-DMUST_MATCH` unless `--non-matching` is passed.
Mapped `--max-errors 0` to both `-maxerrors 0` and `-nofail`, which captures the special compiler behavior needed for unlimited/non-failing diagnostic runs.
Separated missing-file/config verbosity from debug build mode, making `--verbose` the control for these warnings rather than overloading debug configuration.
decomp_lessons: Prefer exposing build-mode and compiler-diagnostic toggles through `configure.py` arguments instead of leaving hardcoded flags or TODO comments in shared flag lists.
Default configuration should favor strict matching behavior; nonmatching and WIP code should require explicit opt-in flags such as `--non-matching` and `--wip`.
Keep developer-facing diagnostics configurable without changing the semantic build mode: verbose missing-source/config warnings do not need to be coupled to `config.debug`.

When porting Makefile behavior into Python configuration, preserve the exact Metrowerks flag spellings emitted by the previous build flow, such as `-msgstyle`, `-maxerrors`, `-warn iserror`, `-requireprotos`, and preprocessor defines.
naming_conventions: CLI options use lowercase kebab-case, e.g. `--msg-style`, `--max-errors`, `--warn-all`, `--non-matching`, `--no-require-protos`.
Argparse destination names use snake_case, e.g. `msg_style`, `max_errors`, `warn_all`, `warn_error`, `non_matching`, `require_protos`.
Build defines remain uppercase C preprocessor-style names: `MUST_MATCH`, `WIP`, `DEBUG`, `NDEBUG`, and `VERSION`.
The PR preserves existing Python helper naming around `ProjectConfig`, `MeleeLib`, `SysdolphinLib`, `Matching`, and `NonMatching` rather than introducing new naming schemes.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use `--non-matching` to suppress `-DMUST_MATCH` when compiling nonmatching code paths during incremental decomp work.
Use `--wip` to add `-DWIP` for explicitly gated work-in-progress code.
Use strict defaults, including matching mode and prototype requirements, so normal builds continue to catch accidental divergence.
Compiler diagnostics can be tuned with `--msg-style`, `--max-errors`, `--warn-all`, and `--no-warn-error` without editing `configure.py`.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that add or modify `configure.py` argparse build options.
Search for uses of `--non-matching`, `--wip`, and `--no-require-protos` in documentation or CI scripts.
Check whether Makefile support for these options was removed or kept after this port.
Look for later changes to defaults for `warn_error`, `require_protos`, or matching/nonmatching builds.
